trial_details.trajectory:
I'll diagnose and fix the Kubernetes pod "test-client" in namespace "case13-triple". Let me start by examining the pod.Now let me check what services exist in this namespace and investigate the webapp-svc:Perfect! I've identified the problem. The webapp pod is failing to pull the image because of a typo: "nginx:latets" instead of "nginx:latest". Let me check the deployment and fix it:Now let me fix the deployment by correcting the image name:Let me check the deployment spec to find the correct container name:Good! The container name is "nginx". Now let me fix the image:Perfect! The fix has been applied.

## DIAGNOSIS
The "test-client" pod was failing because the backend service "webapp-svc" was unavailable. The webapp pod was unable to start due to an image pull error: the deployment was configured to use "nginx:latets" (a typo) instead of "nginx:latest", causing ImagePullBackOff.

## FIX APPLIED
Fixed the webapp deployment by correcting the container image from "nginx:latets" to "nginx:latest" using:
```
kubectl set image deployment/webapp -n case13-triple nginx=nginx:latest
```

This will trigger a rolling update to deploy the correct nginx image, allowing the service to become available and enabling the test-client pod to complete its connectivity test successfully.

**FIX COMPLETE**
